title: Conflict, Clarity, and Right Action
summary: These opening chapters move from Arjuna's crisis into the first foundations of self-knowledge and disciplined action.
insight_caption: This section moves from emotional collapse to philosophical clarity, showing how duty becomes meaningful when guided by right understanding.

title: Renunciation, Meditation, and Higher Knowledge
summary: These chapters deepen inward discipline, linking renunciation in action, meditation, and the movement toward higher knowledge.
insight_caption: This section develops inward discipline, showing how renunciation, meditation, and knowledge support one another.

title: Devotion, Manifestation, and Divine Vision
summary: The middle arc of the Gita centers on devotion, the recognition of divine presence, and the cosmic vision.
insight_caption: This section gathers the devotional heart of the Gita, where intimacy, manifestation, and divine vision converge.

title: Field, Nature, and Supreme Being
summary: These chapters clarify the field of experience, the workings of nature, and the deeper source beyond them both.
insight_caption: This section refines metaphysical understanding by distinguishing body, nature, qualities, and the supreme person.

title: Character, Faith, and Liberation
summary: The final chapters gather ethics, faith, renunciation, and final liberation into one closing movement.
insight_caption: This final section turns the teaching toward lived character, the quality of faith, and the integrated path to liberation.

title: Foundations of Responsible Action
summary: This section gathers the opening chapters where duty, self-command, and responsible action are first laid down.
insight_caption: This selection focuses on the move from confusion to clarity and on the disciplined logic of action without attachment.

title: Renunciation Within Action
summary: These chapters show how action and renunciation are reconciled through meditation, discipline, and steadiness.
insight_caption: This section shows that the path of action matures through inner renunciation and meditative steadiness.

title: Steady Wisdom
summary: Chapter 2 introduces the philosophical backbone of the Gita and grounds the collection in self-knowledge.
insight_caption: This section begins the knowledge path with the classic teaching on the self, equanimity, and steady understanding.

title: Revelation and Higher Knowing
summary: These chapters explore deeper knowledge of the divine and the nature of the imperishable.
insight_caption: This section widens knowledge from inward discrimination into a broader apprehension of the divine and the imperishable.

title: Field, Nature, and the Supreme
summary: These chapters refine the knowledge path through distinctions among body, nature, qualities, and the highest reality.
insight_caption: This selection focuses on metaphysical clarity: the field and knower, the gunas, and the supreme person beyond change.

title: The Devotional Heart of the Gita
summary: These chapters gather the central devotional movement of the text through intimacy, manifestation, and divine vision.
insight_caption: This section traces how devotion moves from trust and intimacy into cosmic vision and loving devotion.

title: Surrender and Final Integration
summary: The closing chapter gathers devotion into a final act of surrender and integrated spiritual understanding.
insight_caption: This section closes the devotional path by showing surrender as the culmination of knowledge, action, and trust.
